Head of government of Tokyo

The **governor of Tokyo** (東京都知事, *Tōkyō-To Chiji*) is the head of government of [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo").

In 1943, upon the unification of [Tokyo City](/wiki/Tokyo_City "Tokyo City") and [Tokyo Prefecture](/wiki/Tokyo_Prefecture_(1868%E2%80%931943) "Tokyo Prefecture (1868–1943)") into [Tokyo Metropolitan Government](/wiki/Tokyo_Metropolitan_Government "Tokyo Metropolitan Government"), the position of Governor was created. Previously, the prefecture had a Governor while the City had [a Mayor](/wiki/Mayors_of_Tokyo "Mayors of Tokyo"). The current title was adopted in 1947 due to the enactment of the Local Autonomy Law.

## Overview

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Governor_of_Tokyo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Overview")]

The Governor of Tokyo is the head of the [Tokyo Metropolitan Government](/wiki/Tokyo_Metropolitan_Government "Tokyo Metropolitan Government"), and is elected by the citizens of [Tokyo Metropolis](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo") every four years, most recently in the [2024 Tokyo gubernatorial election](/wiki/2024_Tokyo_gubernatorial_election "2024 Tokyo gubernatorial election").

As [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo") has the largest economy and population in the country, government policies can greatly affect national affairs, giving a governor significant influence in the country and a louder voice in the National Governors' Association.

The annual budget of [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo") is about 13 trillion yen, ten times that of other prefectures and comparable to the national budget of [Indonesia](/wiki/Indonesia "Indonesia"), so a governor also has a great deal of influence in the [national economy](/wiki/Economy_of_Japan "Economy of Japan").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The [Tokyo Metropolitan Government](/wiki/Tokyo_Metropolitan_Government "Tokyo Metropolitan Government") has more than 160,000 employees, making it by far Japan's largest employer.[[2]](#cite_note-auto-2)

## Qualifications

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Governor_of_Tokyo&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Qualifications")]

Candidates must be a citizen of [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan") and be a resident of [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo") for more than three months, and must be over the age of 30. Candidates must also put up three million yen to the [Tokyo Metropolitan Government](/wiki/Tokyo_Metropolitan_Government "Tokyo Metropolitan Government"), which will only be returned if they receive at least 10% of the votes.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## List

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Governor_of_Tokyo&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: List")]

Main article: [Tokyo Metropolitan Government § Governor](/wiki/Tokyo_Metropolitan_Government#Governor "Tokyo Metropolitan Government")

| 2 |  |  | **[Ryutaro Azuma](/wiki/Ryotaro_Azuma "Ryotaro Azuma")** (1893–1983) | April 27, 1959 | April 22, 1967 | [Independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician")[[a]](#cite_note-a-4) | 1959  ---   1963 |
| 3 |  |  | **[Ryokichi Minobe](/wiki/Ryokichi_Minobe "Ryokichi Minobe")** (1904–1984) | April 23, 1967 | April 22, 1979 | [Independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician")[[b]](#cite_note-b-5) | [1967](/wiki/1967_Tokyo_gubernatorial_election "1967 Tokyo gubernatorial election")   ---   1971   ---   1975 |
| 4 |  |  | [**Shunichi Suzuki**](/wiki/Shunichi_Suzuki_(governor) "Shunichi Suzuki (governor)")  (1910–2010) | April 23, 1979 | April 22, 1995 | [Independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician") | 1979  ---   1983   ---   1987   ---   1991 |
| 5 |  |  | **[Yukio Aoshima](/wiki/Yukio_Aoshima "Yukio Aoshima")**  (1932–2006) | April 23, 1995 | April 22, 1999 | [Independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician") | 1995 |
| 6 |  |  | **[Shintaro Ishihara](/wiki/Shintaro_Ishihara "Shintaro Ishihara")** (1932–2022) | April 23, 1999 | October 31, 2012 | [Independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician")[[c]](#cite_note-c-6) | [1999](/wiki/1999_Tokyo_gubernatorial_election "1999 Tokyo gubernatorial election")   ---   [2003](/wiki/2003_Tokyo_gubernatorial_election "2003 Tokyo gubernatorial election")   ---   [2007](/wiki/2007_Tokyo_gubernatorial_election "2007 Tokyo gubernatorial election")   ---   [2011](/wiki/2011_Tokyo_gubernatorial_election "2011 Tokyo gubernatorial election") |
| 7 |  |  | **[Naoki Inose](/wiki/Naoki_Inose "Naoki Inose")**  (1946-) | December 18, 2012 | December 24, 2013 | [Independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician")[[d]](#cite_note-d-7) | [2012](/wiki/2012_Tokyo_gubernatorial_election "2012 Tokyo gubernatorial election") |
| 8 |  |  | **[Yōichi Masuzoe](/wiki/Y%C5%8Dichi_Masuzoe "Yōichi Masuzoe")**  (1948–) | February 11, 2014 | June 21, 2016 | [Independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician")[[c]](#cite_note-c-6) | [2014](/wiki/2014_Tokyo_gubernatorial_election "2014 Tokyo gubernatorial election") |

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Governor_of_Tokyo&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-a_4-0)** Elected with the support of the LDP.
2. **[^](#cite_ref-b_5-0)** First elected with the support of the Japan Socialist Party and Japan Communist Party.
3. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-c_6-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-c_6-1) Elected with the support of the LDP and New Komeito.
4. **[^](#cite_ref-d_7-0)** Elected with the support of the LDP, New Komeito, and Restoration.
5. **[^](#cite_ref-e_8-0)** Elected with the support of the Tomin First in 2020.
